name: Salem Five Cents Savings Bank
description_short: Salem Five Cents Savings Bank is a Massachusetts-based community bank founded in 1855, offering personal and business banking, mortgages, loans, and wealth management services with FDIC insurance.
description_long:
Salem Five Cents Savings Bank has operated since 1855, making it a well-established regional financial institution headquartered in Salem, Massachusetts. The bank has evolved from a traditional savings bank into a full-service financial institution serving both personal and business customers across multiple market segments.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking products including personal checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, CDs, retirement accounts, mortgages (home purchase, refinance, construction), home equity lines of credit, auto loans, personal credit cards, and digital banking services including mobile apps, online banking, Zelle transfers, and digital wallet capabilities. For business customers, Salem Five provides commercial checking, business savings accounts, SBA loans, lines of credit, real estate financing, construction loans, merchant services, and cash management solutions. They also serve nonprofit organizations and offer commercial banking services including middle-market banking, sponsor finance, and aviation financing.

Salem Five distinguishes itself through its emphasis on community banking relationships and personalized service. The bank highlights its role as a "true partner" with business clients and emphasizes a business network approach. They maintain a physical presence with multiple locations (referenced as "LOCATIONS" on their site) and offer 24/7 online account access. The bank maintains FDIC insurance backing, explicitly stating coverage by "the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government." They also provide educational resources through an Education Center and various financial calculators.

As a regional community bank, Salem Five serves customers seeking traditional banking relationships with local accountability rather than megabank operations. However, customers should note that investment services and securities are offered through separate affiliated entities (LPL Financial) and are not FDIC-insured, which represents a clear separation between traditional banking and investment products. The bank's regional focus means availability is primarily limited to Massachusetts customers.
services: Money market accounts and certificates of deposit (CDs)
Personal credit cards and overdraft protection
SBA loans and business lines of credit
Real estate financing and construction loans
Mobile banking and online banking with Zelle and digital wallet
Wealth management and investment planning services
Wire transfers and remote deposit capture
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by the U.S. Government with explicit federal backing
Operating since 1855 with 168+ years of established banking history and community presence
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, mortgages, business loans, and wealth management all under one institution
24/7 online banking and mobile app access with digital wallet and Zelle integration
Specialized business banking services including SBA loans, middle-market banking, and commercial lending
Educational resources and financial calculators available to help customers make informed decisions
Multiple account types with rate comparison tools to help customers find suitable products
Dedicated support center available at 800-850-5000 with email contact options
cons: Regional limitation: primarily serves Massachusetts customers, limiting accessibility for those outside the state
Investment services offered through separate LPL Financial entities with no FDIC insurance protection
Website navigation complexity with multiple sections (personal, business, commercial) that may confuse some users
Limited information on website regarding specific interest rates, fees, or account minimums
No mention of competitive APR rates or how they compare to national or online banks
best_for: Massachusetts residents and businesses seeking traditional community banking with personal relationships
Small to mid-sized business owners needing comprehensive commercial banking and specialized lending services
Customers who value FDIC insurance and established banking institutions with multi-generational history
Nonprofit organizations requiring dedicated nonprofit banking and cash management services
diagnosis: Salem Five Cents Savings Bank is best suited for Massachusetts-based individuals and businesses seeking a community-oriented bank with deep regional roots and comprehensive financial services. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to Massachusetts and the separation of investment products (which lack FDIC insurance) into affiliated entities, requiring customers to understand the distinction between traditional bank deposits and investment offerings.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
